pi_items.source: Medical literature (StatPearls, MDPI) confirms normal liver is 50-70 HU, while steatosis can drop to ~12 HU.
Medical literature (StatPearls, Singapore Medical Journal) confirms normal spleen is 40-60 HU.
RadiologyKey; clinical thresholds for steatosis are typically <0.8 or <1.0, with normal >1.1.

privileged_info_summary: The data for case amos_0001 is synthetic, generated with a simulated liver (HU ~60) and spleen (HU ~55). In clinical practice, liver attenuation on non-contrast CT typically ranges from 10 to 100 HU (covering both normal tissue and steatosis), and spleen attenuation ranges from 30 to 80 HU. The diagnostic liver-to-spleen (L/S) ratio range is verified as 0.5 to 1.5, where values below 0.8-1.0 suggest hepatic steatosis and values above 1.1 are considered normal.
